perturbation.original_text: 4.18 Party B shall establish a "black list" policy for customer service, based on which Party B will include the users who maliciously owe fees or customize services into a "black list" for management, and Party B shall well explain such policy to relevant users. Party B shall use technical means to monitor the extraordinary traffic, and implement monitor alert and restriction on any owed fees that may be produced by large amount of information fee (over RMB 150/day/user) arising from malicious customization and ordering of services. Party B shall take restrictive measures on the canceled or recovered user numbers provided by Party A every day, and take restrictive measures on the canceled or empty numbers every month. In case Party B fails to take measures on such users, or fails to take measures in time, it shall be responsible for all high-value maliciously owed fees or such other owed fees arising from the using of mobile game services by the users with canceled or suspended number, and Party A will deduct such amounts from the information fee to be settled.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.18 Party B may establish a "black list" policy for customer service, based on which Party B *might* include the users who maliciously owe fees or customize services into a "black list" for management, and Party B *should* explain such policy to relevant users. Party B *may* use technical means to monitor the extraordinary traffic, and *may consider* implementing monitor alert and restriction on any owed fees that may be produced by large amount of information fee (over RMB 150/day/user) arising from malicious customization and ordering of services. Party B *may take* restrictive measures on the canceled or recovered user numbers provided by Party A every day, and *may take* restrictive measures on the canceled or empty numbers every month. In case Party B fails to take measures on such users, or fails to take measures in time, it *might* be responsible for all high-value maliciously owed fees or such other owed fees arising from the using of mobile game services by the users with canceled or suspended number, and Party A *might* deduct such amounts from the information fee to be settled.
perturbation.explanation: By changing shall to may, and replacing the strong enforcement wording, this removes the obligation for Party B to have a blacklist policy. This introduces a contradiction because while other parts of the contract assume this is a mandatory requirement, this section now makes it optional. This impacts section 7.14 where abnormal consumption is to be written off with Party B's help, implying a blacklist exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.14 The abnormal consumption that may be written off with the help of Party B shall be deducted from the Settled Information Service Fee of the current period after confirmed by the Parties, and Party A shall refund such fee to the relevant users.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'may' and the inclusion of wording such as 'might' and 'may consider' in Section 4.18 transforms what was a mandatory obligation for Party B to establish and maintain a blacklist policy and take restrictive measures, into a discretionary option. This directly contradicts Section 7.14, which assumes the existence of Party B's help in writing off abnormal consumption from a blacklist. With Section 4.18 now permissive rather than obligatory, Party B might not have a blacklist, making it unclear how 'the abnormal consumption that may be written off with the help of Party B' in Section 7.14 can be achieved, creating a strong contradiction and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Party B shall be responsible for content development, platform building and maintenance, marketing and customer service of the cooperation business.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Party B is generally responsible for content development and marketing of the cooperation business. The details regarding maintenance and customer service are as outlined in Appendix B.
perturbation.explanation: By stating "details regarding maintenance and customer service are as outlined in Appendix B," but then failing to include Appendix B in the contract, it is now impossible to determine who is responsible for those actions. This contradicts the idea throughout the document (like 5.2) that Party B has complete responsibility to handle those matters. As a result of this, the legal agreement will create contradiction problems between section 3.1 and section 5.2 because there's no specific requirements for the cutomer service that the Party B should provide.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Party B shall provide standardized and adequate customer service system to deal with the consultation, claims and complaints by users with respect to the relevant application services or contents it provides. Such system shall include, without limitation: (1) Customer service call: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week in service, which shall be service numbers starting with 800 or 4008, and shall notbe ordinary in-city number or mobile number; (2) Platform functions: customer systems having such functions as exchange, automatic call distribution, computer telephony integration,interactive voice response, manual agent, recording, data base, business preposition, etc.; (3) Business functions: customer service center will have mainly functions represented by in-calls and manual service, such as informationinquiries, business cancellation, business consulting and complaint handling; (4) Seat placing: SP's customer service shall have at least 5 seats and 12 in-call customer service staff; (5) System performance and business targets: call completion rate by the system: 99% and level of service (call completion rate by manualservice in 20 seconds): 80%; (6) Email box for complaints.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 3.1 removes the explicit responsibility of Party B for 'maintenance and customer service' and vaguely refers to 'Appendix B,' which is not included. This directly contradicts section 5.2, which details specific and mandatory customer service obligations for Party B, including 24/7 service, specific service numbers, platform functions, business functions, staffing levels, and system performance metrics. The omission of Appendix B and the altered responsibility create a strong contradiction, making it unclear whether Party B is obligated to fulfill the requirements outlined in 5.2, rendering that section unenforceable as it relates to the undefined details of Appendix B.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Upon negotiations between the Parties, Party B will pay Party A RMB three thousand (3,000) as performance bond after this Agreement comes into force.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The need of a performance bond will be decided at a later time. When and if that's the case, Party B will pay Party A RMB three thousand (3,000) as performance bond after this Agreement comes into force.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition to say that the performence bond might not be needed, this creates an in-text contradition because in section 7.6 it's stated that Party B has to pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 when requesting the payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.6 The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month. When requesting payment of relevant contract price from Party A, Party B has to: (1) provide official invoices that are in compliance with the requirements of State tax laws by the 25t h of the second month (Party B, instead of any of its affiliates, shall be the invoice issuer); (2) provide the payment request confirmed by the Parties; and (3) pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.1 makes the performance bond optional, while section 7.6 states that Party B "has to... pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 hereof" when requesting payment. This creates a direct contradiction, because if the performance bond is optional as dictated by 6.1, then party B does not necessarily "have to" pay it.
